opinion.h: BURNHAM VAN SERVICE v. PENTECOST, 382 U.S. 106 (1965)
BURNHAM VAN SERVICE ET AL. v. PENTECOST ET AL. APPEAL FROM THE SUPREME COURT OF TENNESSEE. No. 494. Decided November 15, 1965.
opinion.p: J. G. Lackey, Jr., for appellants.
George F. McCanless, Attorney General of Tennessee, and Milton P. Rice, Assistant Attorney General, for appellees.
The motion to dismiss is granted and the appeal is dismissed for want of a substantial federal question.
opinion.h: ALABAMA HIGHWAY EXPRESS, INC. v. UNITED STATES, 382 U.S. 106 (1965)
opinion.p: 382 U.S. 106 ALABAMA HIGHWAY EXPRESS, INC., ET AL. v. UNITED STATES ET AL. APPEAL FROM THE UNITED STATES DISTRICT COURT FOR THE NORTHERN DISTRICT OF ALABAMA. No. 447. Decided November 15, 1965.
James W. Wrape and Robert E. Joyner for appellants.
Solicitor General Marshall, Assistant Attorney General Turner, Robert B. Hummel, Robert W. Ginnane and Robert S. Burk for the United States et al.
The motion to affirm is granted and the judgment is affirmed.
